32.reason: [searchbst uses global count1 which is not reset before each call, and relies on exact match in BST which may have duplicates; also, height calculation logic is flawed because it counts nodes during search instead of storing height during insertion.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [searchbst uses global count1 which is not reset before each call, and relies on exact match in BST which may have duplicates; also, height calculation logic is flawed because it counts nodes during search instead of storing height during insertion.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.2 , reason:  [searchbst uses global count1 which is not reset before each call, causing incorrect height calculation. Also, count1 is only reset upon finding the key, but if the key is not found (shouldn't happen), it remains incremented.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.16666666666666666 , reason:  [searchbst resets count1 only after finding the key, but count1 is global and not reset before each call, causing incorrect height calculation]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.2 , reason:  [The function searchbst uses a global count1 that is not reset properly across calls, and it assumes the key exists and will be found exactly once, which may not hold if duplicates exist. Also, count1 is not initialized before each search.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.25 , reason:  [The function searchbst uses global variable count1 which is not reset properly before each call, and it assumes the key exists exactly once, but duplicates may exist and traversal path may not reflect actual insertion height. Also, count1 is not initialized before each search.]

76.reason: [count1 is a global variable that accumulates across calls; it should be local or passed as parameter to correctly compute height per search. Also, this approach does not guarantee correct height if duplicate keys exist and the tree structure causes different paths.]
